name: Mock Test - 07
instruction: The Question Paper contains three sections. Section A has 25 questions. Attempt any 20 questions. Section B has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions. Section C has 6 questions. Attempt any 5 questions. All questions carry equal marks. There is no negative marking.

questions.question_html:
In the following reaction, the compound used in the reaction for synthesizing ethyl fluoride is:
________ +AgF {tex}\to{/tex} H3C – F + AgBr

options.text: H 3 C – Br,Free radical fluorination of methane
options.html: H<sub>3</sub>C – Br,Free radical fluorination of methane

questions.explanation: Alkyl fluorides are only formed by Swartz's reaction. In Swartz reaction, alkyl chlorides or bromide reacts with metallic flouride AgF to form alkyl fluorides.

questions.question_text: The two hormones which together regulate the glucose level in the blood
questions.question_html: The two hormones which together regulate the glucose level in the blood
questions.explanation: The role of insulin is to keep the blood glucose level within the narrow limit. Insulin is released in response to the rapid rise in blood glucose level. On the other hand hormone glucagon tends to increase the glucose level in the blood. The two hormones together regulate the glucose level in the blood.

questions.question_text: What is the coordination number in a square close-packed structure in two dimensions?
questions.question_html: What is the coordination number in a square close-packed structure in two dimensions?
questions.explanation: Two-dimension close-packed structures, where rows of identical spherical molecules are stacked on top of each other, can be done in two ways, square and hexagonal. In a square close-packed structure, the second row is stacked just above the first row, and the spheres are aligned horizontally and vertically as the way mentioned above. If we observe a sphere in this arrangement, it is surrounded by four spheres that are in direct contact with it. Joining the centers of the four spheres also forms a square, thus giving the term square close-packed structure in two- dimension. Thus by definition of coordination number, a sphere in close-packed structure is 4.

questions.question_text: If two liquids A and B form minimum boiling azeotrope at some specific composition then ________.
questions.question_html: If two liquids A and B form minimum boiling azeotrope at some specific composition then ________.

options.text: A-B interactions are weaker than those between A-A or B-B.
options.html: A-B interactions are weaker than those between A-A or B-B.

options.text: A-B interactions are stronger than those between A-A or B-B.
options.html: A-B interactions are stronger than those between A-A or B-B.

options.text: vapour pressure of solution increases because more number of molecules of liquids A and B can escape from the solution.
options.html: vapour pressure of solution increases because more number of molecules of liquids A and B can escape from the solution.

options.text: vapour pressure of solution decreases because less number of molecules of only one of the liquids escape from the solution.
options.html: vapour pressure of solution decreases because less number of molecules of only one of the liquids escape from the solution.

questions.correct_option_text: A-B interactions are weaker than those between A-A or B-B.
questions.explanation: If A-B interactions will be weaker than A-A or B-B interaction then the result will be a positive deviation. The solutions show a large positive deviation from minimum boiling azeotropes.

questions.question_text: The geometry of XeOF 2 is:
questions.question_html: The geometry of XeOF2 is:
questions.explanation: It has 1 pi bond. Total number of electron pairs is 6 and 2 lone pairs. So it is sp 3 d hybridised and has T shaped geometry.

questions.question_text: Which of the carbon atoms present in the molecule given below are asymmetric?
questions.question_html: Which of the carbon atoms present in the molecule given below are asymmetric?
questions.explanation: Chiral molecules(the object which is non-superimposable on their mirror image) contains one carbon atom surrounded by four non-identical species. All straight-chain molecules cannot be chiral because of the presence of two or more identical groups like hydrogens. Even the carbons with double or triple bonds to a group cannot be considered as a chiral carbon. An asymmetric carbon needs to be surrounded by four species different from each other through covalent bonds. Hence, the atoms b and c are asymmetric.

questions.question_text: Reactions with iodine in preparation of aryl iodide from arenes require the presence of_______.
questions.question_html: Reactions with iodine in preparation of aryl iodide from arenes require the presence of_______.
questions.explanation: Reactions with iodine are reversible in nature and require the presence of an oxidizing agent (HNO 3 , HIO 4 ) to oxidize the HI formed during iodination.

questions.question_text: In which one of the following options, both compounds have a sweet taste?
questions.question_html: In which one of the following options, both compounds have a sweet taste?
questions.explanation: Both glucose and glycine have sweet taste.

questions.question_text: Which of the following point defects are shown by AgBr(s) crystals? Schottky defect Frenkel defect Metal excess defect Metal deficiency defect
questions.question_html:
Which of the following point defects are shown by AgBr(s) crystals?

  1. Schottky defect
  2. Frenkel defect
  3. Metal excess defect
  4. Metal deficiency defect
questions.explanation: AgBr shows both Schottky as well as Frenkel defect. In AgBr, both Ag + and Br - ions are absent from the lattice causing Schottky defect. However, Ag+ions are mobile so they have a tendency to move aside the lattice and trapped in interstitial site hence, causing dislocation defect (Frenkel defect).

questions.question_text: Solubility of gas decreases with
questions.question_html: Solubility of gas decreases with
questions.explanation: Because of increase in kinetic energy of gas molecules with temperature their tendency to escape from the liquid will increase.

questions.question_html: A radioactive element which can decay to give two noble gases is:
questions.explanation: {tex}\cdot_{88}^{226} R a \rightarrow \cdot_{2}^{4} H e+._{86}^{22} R n{/tex}

questions.question_text: Methyl bromide is converted into ethane by heating it in ether medium with:
questions.question_html: Methyl bromide is converted into ethane by heating it in ether medium with:
questions.explanation: The Wurtz reaction, named after Charles-Adolphe Wurtz, is a coupling reaction in organic chemistry, organometallic chemistry and recently inorganic main group polymers, whereby two alkyl halides are reacted with sodium metal in dry ether solution to form a higher alkane: 2R–X + 2Na {tex}\to {/tex} R–R + 2NaX.

questions.question_text: Reaction of an alcohol with organic acid is called the ____
questions.question_html: Reaction of an alcohol with organic acid is called the ____
questions.explanation: Esterification is the reaction in which a Carboxylic acid combines with an alcohol in the presence of little concentrated sulphuric acid to form an ester. The esters so formed are pleasant smelling.

questions.question_text: Maltose is made of:
questions.question_html: Maltose is made of:
questions.explanation: A disaccharide, maltose is composed of two α-D-glucose units in which C 1 of one glucose (I) is linked to C 4 of another glucose unit (II).

questions.question_text: The correct order of the packing efficiency in different types of unit cells is:
questions.question_html: The correct order of the packing efficiency in different types of unit cells is:
questions.explanation: Packing efficiency (is the percentage of total space filled by the particle) in different types of unit cells can be tabulated as Unit Cell Packing Efficiency fcc 74% bcc 68% Simple Cubic 52.4% Hence, correct order is fcc (74%) > bcc (68%) > simple cubic (52.4%)

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements is false ?
questions.question_html: Which of the following statements is false ?

options.text: The value of molal depression constant depends on the nature of the solvent.
options.html: The value of molal depression constant depends on the nature of the solvent.

options.text: Units of atmospheric pressure and osmotic pressure are the same.
options.html: Units of atmospheric pressure and osmotic pressure are the same.

options.text: In reverse osmosis, solvent molecules move through a semipermeable membrane from a region of lower concentration of solute to a region of higher concentration.
options.html: In reverse osmosis, solvent molecules move through a semipermeable membrane from a region of lower concentration of solute to a region of higher concentration.

options.text: Relative lowering of vapour pressure is a dimensionless quantity.
options.html: Relative lowering of vapour pressure is a dimensionless quantity.

questions.correct_option_text: In reverse osmosis, solvent molecules move through a semipermeable membrane from a region of lower concentration of solute to a region of higher concentration.
questions.explanation: In reverse osmosis, pure solvent molecules move through a semipermeable membrane from a higher concentration of solute to lower concentration. Reverse osmosis is used in the desalination of sea water.

questions.question_text: The crystals of ferrous sulphate on heating gives:
questions.question_html: The crystals of ferrous sulphate on heating gives:

options.text: FeO + SO 3 + H 2 SO 4 + H 2 O
Fe 2 O 3 + SO 2 + SO 3 + H 2 O

questions.correct_option_text: Fe 2 O 3 + SO 2 + SO 3 + H 2 O
questions.explanation: FeSO 4 on heating gives Fe 2 O 3 + SO 2 + SO 3 + H 2 O. At 300 o C, hydrated ferrous sulphate becomes anhydrous and colourless. This salt when strongly heated breaks up to form a ferric oxide with the evolution of SO 2 and SO 3 . FeSO 4 .7H 2 O {tex}\to{/tex} FeSO 4 → Fe 2 O 3 + SO 2 ↑ + SO 3 {tex}\uparrow{/tex}

questions.question_text: Reduction potentials of some ions are given below. Arrange them in decreasing order of oxidising power. Ion {tex}ClO_4^-{/tex} {tex}IO_4^-{/tex} {tex}BrO_4^-{/tex} Reduction potential E/V E = 1.19 V E = 1.65V E = 1.74 V
questions.question_html:
Reduction potentials of some ions are given below. Arrange them in decreasing order of oxidising power.

Ion {tex}ClO_4^-{/tex} {tex}IO_4^-{/tex} {tex}BrO_4^-{/tex}
Reduction potential E/V E = 1.19 V E = 1.65V E = 1.74 V
questions.explanation: The higher the value of reduction potential, the higher is its tendency to get reduced Hence, the order of oxidizing power is {tex}\mathrm{BrO}_{4}^{-}>\mathrm{IO}_{4}^{-}>\mathrm{ClO}_{4}^{-}{/tex}

questions.question_html: 3 - methyl phenol is called
questions.explanation: Meta-Cresol (m-cresol) also 3-methylphenol, is an organic compound with the formula CH 3 C 6 H 4 . It is a colourless, viscous liquid that is an intermediate in the production of other chemicals.

questions.explanation: In the secondary structure of Proteins, a polypeptide chain exists in two forms: Alpha helix - stabilized by a hydrogen bond. Beta pleated sheet.

questions.question_text: Elements of which group are known as ore forming elements?
questions.question_html: Elements of which group are known as ore forming elements?
questions.explanation: A maximum number of ores contain group 16 elements (O, S, etc). They are known as "chalcogens" which was derived from the Greek word chalcos, meaning "ore formers," since they all are be found in copper ores.

questions.question_text: MgO has the structure of NaCl. The coordination number of the ions in MgO is
questions.question_html: MgO has the structure of NaCl. The coordination number of the ions in MgO is
questions.explanation: Coordination Number in NaCl is 6. These are ionic solids. Ions are the constituent particles of ionic solids. Such solids are formed by the three dimensional arrangements of cations and anions bound by strong coulombic (electrostatic) forces.

questions.explanation: I, Il, and III structures have D (dextrorotation) configuration with -OH group on the lowest asymmetric carbon is on the right side which is comparable to (+) glyceraldehyde.

questions.explanation: Urea on reaction with NaOH liberates ammonia. NH 2 CONH 2 + 2NaOH {tex}\to{/tex} Na 2 CO 3 + 2NH 3 {tex}\uparrow{/tex}

questions.question_text: Curdling of milk which is caused due to
questions.question_html: Curdling of milk which is caused due to

options.text: disturbance in primary structure of milk proteins
options.html: disturbance in primary structure of milk proteins

options.text: formation of lactic acid by the bacteria present in milk and resulting denaturation
options.html: formation of lactic acid by the bacteria present in milk and resulting denaturation

questions.correct_option_text: formation of lactic acid by the bacteria present in milk and resulting denaturation
questions.explanation: Curdling of milk is caused due to the formation of lactic acid by the bacteria present in milk. Milk contains proteins. When a protein in its native form, is subjected to physical change like change in temperature or chemical change like change in pH, the hydrogen bonds are disturbed. Due to this, globules unfold and helix get uncoiled and protein loses its biological activity. This is called denaturation of protein.

questions.question_text: Which of the following compounds will react with sodium hydroxide solution in water?
questions.question_html: Which of the following compounds will react with sodium hydroxide solution in water?
questions.explanation: Phenol is more soluble in NaOH than in water is because phenol is slightly more acidic than alcohols. The Ka for phenol in water is 1e-10 which is not very strong. But by mixing with NaOH, it causes the phenol to release the H+ to form sodium phenoxide.

questions.question_text: The isotonic solution have:
questions.question_html: The isotonic solution have:
questions.explanation: Isotonic solutions contain equal concentrations of impermeable solutes on either side of the membrane and so the cell neither swells nor shrinks.

questions.question_html: Which among the following forms basic oxide?
questions.explanation: As we move down the group in the periodic table, the metallic character increases so Bi is a metal thus its oxide is basic.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is true about the value of refractive index of quartz glass?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is true about the value of refractive index of quartz glass?
questions.explanation: Since quartz glass in an amorphous solid having short-range order of constituents in which the regular and periodically repeating pattern is observed over a short distance. Hence the value of the refractive index is the same in all directions, can be measured and not be equal to zero.

questions.question_text: The reaction C 2 H 5 ONa + C 2 H 5 I {tex}\to{/tex} C 2 H 5 OC 2 H 5 + NaI is known as:
questions.question_html: The reaction C2H5ONa + C2H5I {tex}\to{/tex} C2H5OC2H5 + NaI is known as:
questions.explanation: Williamson’s synthesis : When an alkyl halide reacts with sodium alkoxide, ether is formed. This reaction is known as Williamson’s synthesis. The reaction generally follows the S N 2 mechanism for primary alcohols. {tex}\mathop {R - X + R' - \underset{\raise0.3em\hbox{$\smash{\scriptscriptstyle\cdot\cdot}$}}{\ddot O} \mathop {Na}\limits^ + \to R - \underset{\raise0.3em\hbox{$\smash{\scriptscriptstyle\cdot\cdot}$}}{\ddot O} - R' + Na\;X}\limits_{Williamson's\;synthesis} {/tex}

questions.question_text: A metal has a body centered cubic crystal structure. The density of the metal is 5.96 g/cm 3 . Find the volume of the unit cell if atomic mass of metal is 50.
questions.question_html: A metal has a body centered cubic crystal structure. The density of the metal is 5.96 g/cm3. Find the volume of the unit cell if atomic mass of metal is 50.
questions.explanation: {tex}{a^3} = \frac{{M \times Z}}{{{N_A} \times d}}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{50 \times 2}}{{6.02 \times {{10}^{23}} \times 5.96}}{/tex} {tex} = 2.787 \times {10^{ - 23}}c{m^3}{/tex}

questions.question_text: 0.1 M solution of urea, at a given temperature, is isotonic with:
questions.question_html: 0.1 M solution of urea, at a given temperature, is isotonic with:
questions.explanation: 0.1 M solution of urea, at a given temperature, is isotonic with 0.1 M glucose solution as both are non electrolytic.

questions.question_text: Which compound is used in the preparation of caprolactam?
questions.question_html: Which compound is used in the preparation of caprolactam?
questions.explanation: NH 3 is used in the preparation of caprolactam. Caprolactam is an organic compound with the formula (CH 2 ) 5 C(O)NH. It is used to make Nylon 6 fibre and plastics.

questions.question_text: Give IUPAC name of the compound given below. {tex}C{H_3} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits_| H}\limits_{Cl\;\;} - C{H_2} - C{H_2} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits_| H}\limits_{OH} - C{H_3}{/tex}
questions.question_html:
Give IUPAC name of the compound given below.

{tex}C{H_3} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits_| H}\limits_{Cl\;\;} - C{H_2} - C{H_2} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits_| H}\limits_{OH} - C{H_3}{/tex}
questions.explanation: {tex}\mathop {\mathop {C{H_3}}\limits^6 \mathop {\mathop {{\text{ }}C}\limits_|^5 H}\limits_{CL} - \mathop {C{H_2}}\limits^4 - \mathop {C{H_2}}\limits^3 - \mathop {\mathop {{\text{ }}C}\limits_|^2 H}\limits_{OH\;} - \mathop {{\text{ }}C}\limits^1 {H_2}}\limits_{5 - chlorohexan - 2 - ol} {/tex} -OH is given preference over -Cl so numbering is done so that –OH gets the lowest number.

questions.question_text: Which compound is used as the cooling liquid in refrigerators?
questions.question_html: Which compound is used as the cooling liquid in refrigerators?
questions.explanation: Liquid NH 3 is used as a refrigerant. evaporation of a liquid needs heat energy. When liquid ammonia vapourises, it absorbs large quantities of heat without changing its temperature.

questions.question_text: Hydrolysis of starch yields
questions.question_html: Hydrolysis of starch yields
questions.explanation: Glucose is the monomer of many of the larger carbohydrates, namely starch, cellulose. Hydrolysis of starch gives glucose.

questions.question_html:

Reason (R): The reaction occurs by SN2 mechanism.

questions.correct_option_text: A is true but R is false.
questions.explanation: In case of optically active alkyl halides, S N 1 reactions are accompanied by racemisation. The carbocation formed is sp 2 hybridised and planar. The attack of the nucleophile may be accomplished from either side resulting in a mixture of products with opposite configuration i.e., racemic mixture.

questions.question_text: In the given question, arrange the compounds in increasing order of rate of reaction towards nucleophilic substitution.
questions.question_html: In the given question, arrange the compounds in increasing order of rate of reaction towards nucleophilic substitution.
questions.explanation: Electron releasing group increase the reactivity of aryl halides, less is the number of electrons releasing group, the less is rate towards nucleophilic substitution reaction.

questions.question_text: Which of the following reagents can be used to oxidise primary alcohols to aldehydes?
questions.question_html: Which of the following reagents can be used to oxidise primary alcohols to aldehydes?

options.text: Heat in the presence of Cu at 573K.
options.html: Heat in the presence of Cu at 573K.

questions.explanation: Oxidation of alcohols to aldehydes is partial oxidation ; aldehydes are further oxidized to carboxylic acids. Conditions required for making aldehydes are heat and distillation. In aldehyde formation, the temperature of the reaction should be kept above the boiling point of the aldehyde and below the boiling point of the alcohol. These include: Chromium-based reagents, such as Collins reagent (CrO 3 ·Py 2 ) PDC or PCC. Heat in the presence of Cu at 573K.

questions.question_text: Instructions for Question 53 to 55: When haloalkanes with {tex}\beta{/tex} -hydrogen atom are boiled with alcoholic solution of KOH, they undergo elimination of hydrogen halide resulting in the formation of alkenes. These reactions are called {tex}\beta{/tex} -elimination reactions or dehydrohalogenation reactions. These reactions follow Saytzeff's rule. Substitution and elimination reactions often compete with each other. Mostly bases behave as nudeophiles and therefore can engage in substitution or elimination reactions depending upon the alkyl halide and the reaction conditions. The general reaction, {tex}R-X \stackrel{\text { aq. } O H^{-}}{\longrightarrow} R O H+X^{-}{/tex} , is expected to follow decreasing order of reactivity as in
questions.question_html:
Instructions for Question 53 to 55:

When haloalkanes with {tex}\beta{/tex}-hydrogen atom are boiled with alcoholic solution of KOH, they undergo elimination of hydrogen halide resulting in the formation of alkenes. These reactions are called {tex}\beta{/tex}-elimination reactions or dehydrohalogenation reactions. These reactions follow Saytzeff's rule. Substitution and elimination reactions often compete with each other. Mostly bases behave as nudeophiles and therefore can engage in substitution or elimination reactions depending upon the alkyl halide and the reaction conditions.


The general reaction, {tex}R-X \stackrel{\text { aq. } O H^{-}}{\longrightarrow} R O H+X^{-}{/tex}, is expected to follow decreasing order of reactivity as in

options.text: t-BuBr > t-BuCl > t-BuI > t-BuF (t-Bu= tertiary Butyl group)
options.html: t-BuBr > t-BuCl > t-BuI > t-BuF (t-Bu= tertiary Butyl group)

questions.explanation: The order of reactivity of alkyl halides: iodide > bromide > chloride (nature of the halogen atom) tertiary > secondary > primary (type of halogen atom).

questions.question_text: Instructions for Question 53 to 55: When haloalkanes with {tex}\beta{/tex} -hydrogen atom are boiled with alcoholic solution of KOH, they undergo elimination of hydrogen halide resulting in the formation of alkenes. These reactions are called {tex}\beta{/tex} -elimination reactions or dehydrohalogenation reactions. These reactions follow Saytzeff's rule. Substitution and elimination reactions often compete with each other. Mostly bases behave as nudeophiles and therefore can engage in substitution or elimination reactions depending upon the alkyl halide and the reaction conditions. Instructions for Question 53 to 55:

When haloalkanes with {tex}\beta{/tex}-hydrogen atom are boiled with alcoholic solution of KOH, they undergo elimination of hydrogen halide resulting in the formation of alkenes. These reactions are called {tex}\beta{/tex}-elimination reactions or dehydrohalogenation reactions. These reactions follow Saytzeff's rule. Substitution and elimination reactions often compete with each other. Mostly bases behave as nudeophiles and therefore can engage in substitution or elimination reactions depending upon the alkyl halide and the reaction conditions.

questions.explanation: The order of bond dissociation energy: R - F > R - Cl > R - Br > R - I. During dehydrohalogenation C - I bond breaks more easily than C - F bond. So reactivity order of halides R - I > R - Br > R - Cl > R - F
